assets.name: User Credentials (email + MD5 password hash)
assets.description: All registered user email addresses and MD5-hashed passwords stored in SQLite Users table. MD5 provides no practical protection — precomputed rainbow tables can reverse most hashes in seconds. Also includes TOTP secrets for 2FA-enrolled users.

assets.name: RSA Private Key (JWT signing key)
assets.description: 1024-bit RSA private key hardcoded at lib/insecurity.ts:23. Used to sign all JWTs. Committed to public GitHub repository — permanently compromised. Any party who has read the source code can forge valid admin tokens.

assets.name: Active JWT Session Tokens
assets.description: 6-hour RS256-signed JWTs stored in the client-side localStorage and tracked server-side in the authenticatedUsers in-memory map. Tokens encode user ID, role, and basket ID. Forgeable due to committed private key.

assets.name: Customer Order History and Basket Contents
assets.description: SQLite tables for Orders, Baskets, BasketItems including order line items, prices, addresses, and payment card last-four digits. Subject to IDOR attacks via missing authorization on order-history and basket endpoints.

assets.name: User Profile Data (addresses, payment cards, memories)
assets.description: Personal data including delivery addresses, stored payment card references, profile images, and user-created memories. Accessible via IDOR-vulnerable address/payment APIs and served without proper authorization checks.

assets.name: Uploaded Files (profile images, XML, YAML submissions)
assets.description: User-uploaded files stored in uploads/. Includes profile images (SSRF via URL upload), and XML/YAML files processed by the fileUpload handler with XXE enabled. Files served back without strict content-type enforcement.

assets.name: FTP Directory (public download area)
assets.description: The ftp/ directory served with serve-index directory listing at /ftp. Contains legal.md (removed), acquisition files, and a quarantine subdirectory. Path traversal in quarantine server may expose non-public files.

assets.name: Encryption Keys Directory (jwt.pub, premium.key)
assets.description: The encryptionkeys/ directory served publicly via keyServer.ts. Contains jwt.pub (RSA public key) and premium.key. Serving these files publicly exposes the public key for offline JWT analysis.

assets.name: Application Configuration and Version Information
assets.description: Unauthenticated endpoints /rest/admin/application-configuration and /rest/admin/application-version expose detailed runtime configuration including feature flags, domain name, challenge status, and deployed version. Useful for targeted exploitation.

assets.name: Access Logs
assets.description: Morgan combined-format access logs written to logs/. Served via logfileServer.ts with user-supplied filename parameter. Path traversal in the log server could expose other filesystem contents.

assets.name: Challenge Solution State
assets.description: In-memory and database-backed challenge completion flags tracking which OWASP vulnerabilities have been solved. Can be manipulated via continue-code endpoints that lack authentication.

assets.name: Prometheus Metrics Endpoint
assets.description: Unauthenticated /metrics endpoint exposing Express application internals including request counts, response times, endpoint mapping, and runtime behavior — useful for reconnaissance.
